subject: [PATCH v2 00/14] fuse: add io-uring buffer rings and zero-copy

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a concern about duplicated logic in the fuse_uring_send_next_to_ring() function, which contained duplicated logic from fuse_uring_send(). The author has simplified the logic by decoupling request fetching from the send operation and reducing unnecessary parameter passing. A new function fuse_uring_get_next_fuse_req() has been introduced to get the next fuse req, and the existing code has been refactored accordingly.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the copy_to_user() logic being scattered throughout the code, agreeing to centralize it in a new function called copy_header_to_ring(). The patch includes this new function and updates several places where copy_to_user() was used. A fix is planned for v2.
sentiment_signals: planned to address in future version

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed a concern about the copy_from_user() logic in fuse_uring.c by moving it into a new function, copy_header_from_ring(), to centralize error handling and rate-limited logging.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the interface for copying headers in buffer rings, agreeing to use enum types to identify which part of the header needs to be copied, and proposed changes to simplify both kernel-space and user-space header addresses copying.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the import_ubuf() function being called multiple times in fuse_uring_copy_from_ring() and fuse_uring_args_to_ring(). They added a new helper function setup_fuse_copy_state() to contain the logic for setting up the copy state, which replaces these calls. The patch will need further revision.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about how to handle kaddr in fuse_copy_state, explaining that it's used for direct memcpy and adding a check to ensure len is non-zero before returning. The patch will need further revision.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about magic indices in the iovec array by replacing them with named constants FUSE_URING_IOV_HEADERS and FUSE_URING_IOV_PAYLOAD, making the code self-documenting and preparing for future buffer ring support.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about exposing implementation details in the header file by moving the fuse_uring_abort() function to dev_uring.c, where it belongs. The author agreed that this change will make subsequent commits cleaner and is a necessary step for adding pinning capabilities.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the placement of the fuse_uring_get_iovec_from_sqe() function in the file, agreeing to move it earlier for easier review of subsequent commits that add buffer rings.

reviews.author: Bernd Schubert
reviews.summary: The reviewer noted that the code is duplicating functionality from io_pin_pages() and suggested exporting it for use in this patch, also planning to apply a similar technique in ublk.
reviews.raw_body:
I think this is a duplicate of io_pin_pages(), can we just export that
and use here? I'm basically going to propose to use the same technique
in ublk - would be another duplicate.


(Not a complete a review yet, just something I just noticed).


Thanks,
Bernd

reviews.author: Bernd Schubert
reviews.summary: Reviewer Bernd Schubert suggested modifying the fuse implementation to allow SQEs (Submission Queue Entries) with different iovecs for the payload buffer, enabling fuse-server to handle multiple IO sizes by specifying dedicated IO size per pBuf.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Joanne,

thanks a lot for this document! Could we discuss if we could just hook
in here and allow SQEs with different iovecs for the payload buffer?
Let's say fuse-server wants multiple IO sizes - it could easily do that
via different pBufs and just needs to specify the dedicated IO size per
pBuf. Those buffers could then get sorted into an array - we could
define either via FUSE init the number of buf sizes or use a fixed size
array. Fuse requests then would just need to pick the right array.
This is basically what I'm currently working on for ublk.

I think it would be good to agree on the design before it gets merged so
that uapi doesn't change.

Thanks,
Bernd

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author Joanne Koong is addressing a concern about the placement of io_pin_pages() logic, suggesting it should be moved to the mm layer as it's not specific to io-uring. She asks for clarification on whether the memory being passed in is user-allocated and not part of io-uring.
reviews.raw_body:
Tbh I think this is generic logic that makes more sense to live in the
mm layer instead of fuse calling this as an exported io-uring
function. The memory it's passing in is not related to io-uring, so
that was my hesitation. For your ublk use case, is the memory you're
passing into this user-allocated memory that's not part of io-uring?
If so, then maybe it's best to move io_pin_pages() out of io-uring and
into generic mm.

Thanks,
Joanne

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary:
Author responded to Bernd's feedback on fuse server preferring static preallocation over incremental buffer consumption by explaining their design choice for supporting multiple IO size payloads, which involves using a fixed-size header and an array of payload buffers with corresponding sizes.

Author is addressing a concern about the struct fuse_uring_cmd_req layout, specifically the placement of the buf_size field to accommodate multiple io size payloads. The author asks for clarification on how many different payload sizes are envisioned and seeks alignment with the reviewer's expectations.

reviews.author: Bernd Schubert
reviews.summary: The reviewer noted that the #else directive in the patch was unnecessary and suggested moving the real function into the C file instead.
reviews.raw_body:
I had it put in there because of

#else /* CONFIG_FUSE_IO_URING */

but sure, we can also move the real function into the c file.


Reviewed-by: Bernd Schubert <bernd@bsbernd.com>

reviews.author: Bernd Schubert
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the original order of checks and allocation was intended, but is willing to accept the change if it's useful for future commits.
reviews.raw_body:
Hmm, goal was to first do the checks and then to allocate, but if the
change is useful for future commits, fine with me.

Reviewed-by: Bernd Schubert <bernd@bsbernd.com>

reviews.author: Bernd Schubert
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Bernd Schubert suggested renaming the variable 'queue_depth' to 'max_queue_depth', and also proposed dynamically registering ring entries when needed, which would make the current variable a maximum value rather than its actual value.

Reviewer noted that the patch does not properly handle the case where a fuse request is interrupted by an IORING_OP_READ_FIXED or IORING_OP_WRITE_FIXED operation, and requested additional error handling to prevent data corruption.
reviews.raw_body:
Could we call this 'max_queue_depth'? I still think that it might be
useful to register ring entries dynamically when needed at some point.
And then this would become a 'max' value and not the actual value.

---

Thanks,
Bernd

reviews.author: Bernd Schubert
reviews.summary:
Reviewer questioned the necessity of registering a large number of buffer ring entries, suggesting that an entry is only useful when carrying data and implying that registering unnecessary entries may be inefficient.

Bernd Schubert noted that the current design allocates headers into a large region without utilizing extra space, but suggested using 4K allocations for headers to enable inlining small requests and making ring buffers useful by reducing metadata payload buffer allocation. He also mentioned that contiguous buffer allocation can be done for entries as well, but it becomes harder with dynamic entry registration.

Bernd Schubert noted that the use of pbuf for dynamic allocation in io-uring buffer rings is unnecessary, as libfuse can dynamically add or remove entries from the ring and their buffers without requiring a fixed number of SQEs/CQEs. He suggested adding a notification type to wake idle entries and removing them, which he believes would be an easy solution.

The reviewer suggested a design change to use per-queue buffer rings with an order map, which would allow the io-uring ring owner to have multiple pools for its own purposes and enable more efficient buffer recycling.

2026-04-02.patch_summary: This patch series adds support for buffer rings and zero-copy to the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) kernel module, utilizing io-uring capabilities. Buffer rings reduce memory usage by pooling payload buffers and selecting them on demand, while also laying the groundwork for pinned buffers that can be easily managed. Zero-copy is an opt-in feature for privileged servers that eliminates memory copies between the kernel and userspace for read/write-heavy operations. The patch series has been tested to show throughput improvements of up to 35% in certain scenarios.
